items.question: ___ I sit here? (informal)
explanations: Correct! 'Can I…?' is the everyday way to ask permission.
'May I…?' is fine but more formal than 'informal' asks for.
'Must I…?' asks about obligation, not permission.

items.question: ___ I come in? (formal, at an office)
explanations: Correct! 'May I…?' is the formal way to ask permission.
'Can I…?' works but is less formal.
'Would I…?' is not used to ask permission.

items.question: 'Could I borrow your charger?' — 'Yes, you ___ .'
explanations: 'You could' sounds like advice, not permission.

items.question: Sorry, you ___ park here — it's for staff only.
explanations: Correct! Refuse permission with 'can't': 'you can't park here'.
'Couldn't' refers to past ability, not this refusal.

items.question: ___ I ask you a question, please? (polite)
explanations: Correct! 'Could I…?' is a polite way to ask permission.
'Will I…?' is not used to ask permission.

items.question: Students ___ use the lab after 6 p.m. (giving permission, formal)
explanations: Correct! Formal permission uses 'may': 'Students may use the lab'.

items.question: Passengers ___ smoke anywhere on the train.
Correct this: 'Can I to leave early today?'
options: Can I to leave early today?
Can to I leave early today?
May I to leave early today?
explanations: Correct! Modals take the base verb with no 'to': 'Can I leave…?'
'Can to I' is the wrong order.
'May I to leave' also wrongly adds 'to'.

items.question: 'May I take your coat?' is an example of…
options: a formal, polite request for permission.
explanations: Correct! 'May I…?' is a formal, polite way to ask.
It asks, rather than gives, permission.

items.question: Which is used to ASK but not to GIVE permission?
explanations: Correct! 'Could' asks politely but isn't used to give permission.
